capability_objective: The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the case named “Mobile Check-In Failure” at “Grove Clinics” and the case named “Address Validation Rejection” at “Harbor Books”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Keep the reply under 75 words, include both stable IDs, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.”
prompt:
The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `case-audit.txt` from full case records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `CASES.md`, then use Pi's genuine shell execution tool to run the local `./casectl` executable for every support-registry lookup; discover its interface from built-in help before accessing case data. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed or audit key, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the case named “Mobile Check-In Failure” at “Grove Clinics” and the case named “Address Validation Rejection” at “Harbor Books”. Your first case-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./casectl` processes, with no other registry operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following case-data assistant action must start both full-record retrievals concurrently as two separate direct `./casectl` processes and wait for both. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take every reported name, location, stable ID, and status for a resolved branch only from its successful full-record retrieval.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter support data, inspect or edit the execution journal, expose unrelated records, or include other full-record fields. Do not invoke any registry operation beyond interface discovery, the two required searches, and eligible full-record retrievals.

`case-audit.txt` must be under 75 whitespace-delimited words and contain exactly four nonblank, newline-terminated sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. Keep the requested case order. Format a resolved record line exactly as `<name> at <location> [<stable ID>] has retrieved status <status>.` Format an unresolved record line exactly as `<requested name> at <requested location> [UNRESOLVED] is unresolved.` The third line must be exactly `The retrieved statuses match: <status>.` when both retrieved statuses are identical, `The retrieved statuses differ: <first status> versus <second status>.` when both are present and differ, or `The status comparison is unavailable.` when either branch is unresolved. The fourth and final line must be exactly `No changes made.` Include no literal angle brackets. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied files unchanged and make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
